perturbation.changed_text: (g) "Products" shall mean all food items produced and sold by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Products' is limited to 'cold breakfast cereals.' The modified definition expands this to 'all food items produced and sold by the Company.' This creates a contradiction because later clauses refer to 'Products' with the initial, limited scope, but now 'Products' encompasses a much wider range of items. This will create a contradiction within section 2, where it discusses the products that the endorsment is based on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Grant of Endorsement Rights. (a) Pey Dirt grants to Company the exclusive right and license to use Manning Identification within the Contract Territory during the Contract Period in connection with the advertisement and promotion by Company of Products in television, radio, print and point of purchase. Pey Dirt also grants to Company, subject to all of the terms and conditions herein, the non-exclusive right to use the Manning Identification in connection with certain merchandise that may be featured on the back panel of the Endorsed Products packaging, said merchandise to be subject to Pey Dirt's sole and exclusive discretion and approval. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, it is specifically agreed that the Manning Identification cannot be used, in whole or in part, in connection with (i) Premium Programs and/or (ii) any multi-media use except for Company's e- commerce site. Pey Dirt expressly agrees that the right to use Manning Identification will not be granted to anyone other than Company for use within the Contract Territory during the Contract Period in connection with the advertisement, promotion and sale of Products. Anything herein to the contrary notwithstanding, Company shall not have the right to distribute photographs of Manning which are larger than 5" x 7".
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Products' narrowly as 'cold breakfast cereals,' aligning with the endorsement rights granted in Section 2, which focuses on the advertisement and promotion of those specific products. The changed text broadens the definition of 'Products' to 'all food items produced and sold by the Company.' This creates a direct contradiction because Section 2 grants endorsement rights specifically for 'Products' (understood as cold breakfast cereals). The expanded definition now includes other food items, making it unclear whether the endorsement rights apply to those additional items, especially the merchandise that may be featured on the back panel of the Endorsed Products packaging, creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting with the original intent of the agreement related to endorsement of cold breakfast cereals only.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Remuneration. (a) Within thirty (30) days following the conclusion of each Contract Year Quarter, Company shall deliver to Pey Dirt an itemized statement setting forth the total shipments of Endorsed Products during said Contract Year Quarter and, at the same time, shall pay to Pey Dirt a royalty with respect to such shipments as hereinafter provided. Such royalties shall be based upon the actual invoice price of such shipments, exclusive only of shipping charges and sales taxes, and shall be at the rate of 8.5% of the total of said invoice prices with a minimum invoice price of $2.50 per box.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Remuneration. (a) Within thirty (30) days following the conclusion of each Contract Year Quarter, the Licensee shall deliver to Pey Dirt an itemized statement setting forth the total shipments of Endorsed Products during said Contract Year Quarter and, at the same time, shall pay to Pey Dirt a royalty with respect to such shipments as hereinafter provided. Such royalties shall be based upon the actual invoice price of such shipments, exclusive only of shipping charges and sales taxes, and shall be at the rate of 8.5% of the total of said invoice prices with a minimum invoice price of $2.50 per box.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Licensee' in this section introduces uncertainty because 'Licensee' is not explicitly defined elsewhere. While it's implied that 'Licensee' refers to Famous Fixins, Inc., the contract never formally establishes this. This is going to contradict section 16, because that section has the company listed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Indemnity. Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, -7- arising out of, or in any way connected with, this Agreement, Company's default hereunder, the negligence, actions, errors or omissions of Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or otherwise involving alleged defects in Company's Products, provided that Company shall be given notice of any such action or claim. Company agrees to provide and maintain, at its own expense, general liability insurance and product liability insurance with limits no less than $3,000,000 and within thirty (30) days from the date hereof, Company will submit to Pey Dirt a fully paid policy or certificate of insurance naming Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning as additional insured parties, requiring that the insurer shall not terminate or materially modify such without written notice to Pey Dirt at least twenty (20) days in advance thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Company' with 'Licensee' in Section 4, while Section 16 refers to 'Company'. Since 'Licensee' is never explicitly defined elsewhere in the contract, this creates ambiguity and a contradiction. Section 16 imposes indemnity obligations on 'Company', but the change in Section 4 casts doubt on who is responsible for royalty payments, which can trigger indemnity if unpaid. This confusion regarding the responsible party creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Indemnity. Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, -7- arising out of, or in any way connected with, this Agreement, Company's default hereunder, the negligence, actions, errors or omissions of Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or otherwise involving alleged defects in Company's Products, provided that Company shall be given notice of any such action or claim. Company agrees to provide and maintain, at its own expense, general liability insurance and product liability insurance with limits no less than $3,000,000 and within thirty (30) days from the date hereof, Company will submit to Pey Dirt a fully paid policy or certificate of insurance naming Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning as additional insured parties, requiring that the insurer shall not terminate or materially modify such without written notice to Pey Dirt at least twenty (20) days in advance thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Indemnity. Famous Fixins agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, -7- arising out of, or in any way connected with, this Agreement, Company's default hereunder, the negligence, actions, errors or omissions of Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or otherwise involving alleged defects in Company's Products, provided that Famous Fixins shall be given notice of any such action or claim. Famous Fixins agrees to provide and maintain, at its own expense, general liability insurance and product liability insurance with limits no less than $3,000,000 and within thirty (30) days from the date hereof, Famous Fixins will submit to Pey Dirt a fully paid policy or certificate of insurance naming Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning as additional insured parties, requiring that the insurer shall not terminate or materially modify such without written notice to Pey Dirt at least twenty (20) days in advance thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the word 'Company' to 'Famous Fixins' specifically in the beginning and end of the section, but keeping the original term within the text creates a contradiction. It's unclear which term carries legal weight, leading to potential disputes over indemnity and insurance obligations. It will contradict 'Company' being specified in multiple areas in the single paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Indemnity. Famous Fixins agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, -7- arising out of, or in any way connected with, this Agreement, Company's default hereunder, the negligence, actions, errors or omissions of Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or otherwise involving alleged defects in Company's Products, provided that Famous Fixins shall be given notice of any such action or claim. Famous Fixins agrees to provide and maintain, at its own expense, general liability insurance and product liability insurance with limits no less than $3,000,000 and within thirty (30) days from the date hereof, Famous Fixins will submit to Pey Dirt a fully paid policy or certificate of insurance naming Pey Dirt, Pey Dirt's agent and Manning as additional insured parties, requiring that the insurer shall not terminate or materially modify such without written notice to Pey Dirt at least twenty (20) days in advance thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Famous Fixins' at the beginning and end of the indemnity clause, but retains 'Company' within the body of the same clause. This creates an in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether 'Company' or 'Famous Fixins' is responsible for the obligations described in the middle of the section, specifically regarding default, negligence, actions or omissions, and insurance requirements. The modified text (Changed Text) directly contradicts itself, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction with Contradicted Text, where 'Company' and 'Famous Fixins' are used together.
